translatedText: He passed away on November 17, 1917.
Born in Saint-Germain-en-Laye, Yvelines, Île-de-France.
Caught the attention of Madame Marie Mauté de Fleurville.
Entered the Conservatoire de Paris at the age of 11.
Composed Piano Trio in G major.
Composed Nuit d'étoiles (Starry Night) and Beau Soir (Beautiful Evening).
Composed Intermezzo for cello and orchestra.
Won the Second Grand Prix de Rome with the cantata Le Gladiateur.
Won the First Grand Prix de Rome with the cantata L'Enfant prodigue.
Composed the cantata L'Enfant prodigue (The Prodigal Son).
Began composing Ariettes oubliées (Forgotten Songs).
Began composing Petite Suite.
Composed the symphonic suite Printemps.
Frequented Mallarmé's salon.
Began composing Cinq poèmes de Charles Baudelaire.
Began composing La Damoiselle élue (The Blessed Damozel).
Began composing Deux Arabesques.
Began composing Fantaisie for piano and orchestra.
Composed Valse romantique and Rêverie.
Began composing Suite bergamasque.
Composed String Quartet in G minor.
Began composing Pelléas et Mélisande.
Composed the symphonic poem Prélude à l'après-midi d'un faune (Prelude to the Afternoon of a Faun).
Began composing Pour le piano.
Began composing Nocturnes.
Began composing Trois Chansons de Bilitis.
Married Rosalie Texier.
Began composing Rhapsodie pour saxophone alto et orchestre.
Achieved international fame with the premiere of the opera Pelléas et Mélisande.
Began composing Images for orchestra.
Composed Estampes.
Began composing the symphonic poem La Mer.
Fell in love with Emma Bardac and left his wife.
Composed Le Roi Lear and L'Isle joyeuse.
Began composing Images (Books 1 & 2).
Began composing Children's Corner.
Dedicated the piano suite Children's Corner to his daughter, Claude-Emma.
Began composing Première rhapsodie for clarinet and orchestra.
Began composing Préludes (Books 1 & 2).
Began composing Jeux.
Composed Syrinx, La Boîte à joujoux, and Trois poèmes de Stéphane Mallarmé.
Composed En blanc et noir, Études, Élégie, Cello Sonata in D minor, and Sonata for flute, viola and harp in F major.
Began composing Violin Sonata in G minor.
Died of cancer in Paris on March 25.
Funeral held on March 28.
Admission to the Conservatoire de Paris
Entered the Conservatoire de Paris, France's premier musical education institution, at the age of 10 and was recognized for his musical talent.
Winning the First Grand Prix de Rome
Won the First Grand Prix de Rome in 1884 with his cantata L'Enfant prodigue.
Gaining International Fame with Pelléas et Mélisande
Achieved international fame with the premiere of his only completed opera, Pelléas et Mélisande, in 1902.
Establishing Impressionist Style
His Impressionist compositional style was solidified with Prélude à l'après-midi d'un faune, based on a poem by Mallarmé.
Development of an Original Musical Style
Developed his own original harmonic and orchestral palette, influenced by Russian and Oriental music, as well as the works of Chopin.
Influence on Future Composers
Exerted a strong influence on countless composers, including Béla Bartók and Olivier Messiaen.
Of which musical movement is Claude Debussy a representative composer?
Claude Debussy is a representative composer of French Impressionist music.
What are Debussy's representative orchestral works?
Debussy's representative orchestral works include Prélude à l'après-midi d'un faune, Nocturnes (1897-1899), and Images (1905-1912).
When was Debussy born and when did he die?
Debussy was born on August 22, 1862, and died on March 25, 1918.
What education did Debussy receive, and what awards did he win?
He entered the Conservatoire de Paris at age 10 and won the First Grand Prix de Rome in 1884 with his cantata L'Enfant prodigue.
What are the characteristics of Debussy's music?
Debussy's music stemmed from a reaction against Wagner and the German musical tradition, seeking new forms instead of the classical symphonic form.
Which literary movement influenced Debussy?
Debussy was strongly influenced by the late 19th-century Symbolism movement in poetry.
January 25, 1882
Born Adeline Virginia Stephen in London.
Exhibited her first symptoms of mental illness after her mother's death.
Began studying history and Greek at King's College London.
Attempted suicide by throwing herself out of a window during her second bout of mental illness following her father's death, but survived.
Married Leonard Woolf.
Published The Voyage Out.
Published Night and Day.
Mrs Dalloway was published and gained great popularity.
To the Lighthouse was well received.
Orlando was well received.
March 28, 1941
Went missing after going for a walk by the River Ouse; her body was found two days later.
Pioneering the Stream of Consciousness Genre
One of the writers who created and perfected the stream of consciousness genre in the novel.
What kind of writer was Virginia Woolf?
She was an English novelist and essayist, and one of the writers who created and perfected the stream of consciousness genre.
What are Virginia Woolf's major works?
Mrs Dalloway (1925), To the Lighthouse (1927), and Orlando (1928) gained great popularity and critical acclaim.
When did Virginia Woolf die?
She went missing on March 28, 1941, after taking a walk by the River Ouse, and was found dead two days later.
To which group did Virginia Woolf belong?
She was a member of the Bloomsbury Group, which was active in the Bloomsbury area of London from 1907 to 1930.
July 3, 1883
Franz Kafka, a Jewish novelist of the Austro-Hungarian Empire, was born.
Obtained a doctorate in law.
